*����������������� Incoming video frames from the first input are cached and indexed according to the frame index coming in on the second input. The second input expects a single channel of the name "frame_stream". The third input expects a single channel of the name "frame_sync".  The "frame_sync" channel will typically come over the network via SyncIn CHOP.  

The "frame_sync" channel is the frame index that the server of the frame index expects all clients to play. If everything works correctly, then all clients will play the same frame at the same time.

*�����������������SubWidgetExt = mod.SubWidgetExt.SubWidgetExt
TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

class ButtonExt(SubWidgetExt):
	"""
	ButtonExt provides all functionality for basic widget button
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.parPrefix = 'Button'
		SubWidgetExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		self.valueIndex = self.ownerComp.digits

		self.types = [
				('Momentary', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.SetValue(1),
							   'onToOff': lambda p: self.SetValue(0)}),
				('Momentary Up', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.SetValue(0),
								  'onToOff': lambda p: self.SetValue(1)}),
				('Toggle Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Toggle()}),
				('Toggle Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Toggle(checkInside=True)}),
				('Toggle Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Toggle()}),
				('Radio Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Radio Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Radio(checkInside=True)}),
				('Radio Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Exclusive Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Exclusive Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Radio(checkInside=True)}),
				('Exclusive Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Commands Only', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.RunOnCommand(),
							   	   'onToOff': lambda p: self.RunOffCommand()}),
				('Commands Only Ignore Value', {
								   'offToOn': lambda p: self.RunOnCommand(),
							   	   'onToOff': lambda p: self.RunOffCommand()})
		]
		self.buttonTypePar = parent.Widget.par.Buttontype
		self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuLabels = [type[0] for type in self.types]
		self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuNames = self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuLabels

	def SetValue(self, value):
		"""
		Sets the value of the widget
		"""
		setattr(self.ownerComp.parent.Widget.par,
										'Value'+str(self.valueIndex), value)

	def GetValue(self):
		"""
		Sets the value of the widget
		"""
		#debug('value change')
		return getattr(self.ownerComp.parent.Widget.par,
										'Value'+str(self.valueIndex))

	def Radio(self, checkInside=False):
		if self.Toggle(checkInside):
			self.updateRadioGroup()

	def Toggle(self, checkInside=False):
		"""
		Sets the value of the button to on when off, and off when on

		:param checkInside: if True, only toggle if the mouse is currently
			inside the ownerComp panel.
		:return: False if skipped from not inside, otherwise True
		"""
		if checkInside and not self.ownerComp.panel.inside:
			return False
		self.SetValue(1-self.GetValue())
		return True

	def RunOnCommand(self):
		"""
		Runs the python command for when the button turns on
		"""
		#debug('command on')
		command = parent.Widget.par.Offtoonscript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
		command = parent.Widget.par.Onvaluechangescript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)

	def RunOffCommand(self):
		"""
		Runs the python command for when the button turns off
		"""
		#debug('command off')
		command = parent.Widget.par.Ontooffscript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
		command = parent.Widget.par.Onvaluechangescript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
	@property
	def GroupButtons(self):
		table = parent.Widget.par.Buttongroupdat.eval()
		if not isinstance(table, DAT):
			return []
		tableParent = table.parent()
		return [tableParent.op(c.val)
							for c in table.col(0) if tableParent.op(c.val)]

	def onPanelEvent(self, event, panelValue=None):
		"""
		Selects correct funtion based on button widget type
		"""
		#debug('panelEvent', event)
		typeIndex = self.buttonTypePar.menuIndex
		btype = self.types[typeIndex][1].get(event)
		#debug(self.types[typeIndex][0])
		if btype:
			#debug('onPanel', event)
			btype(panelValue)

	def onParValueChange(self, par, prev):
		"""
		The Button's value has changed.
		Only runs when necessary (see parexecValue 'Active' parameter).
		"""
		type = self.buttonTypePar.eval()
		if type == 'Commands_Only_Ignore_Value':
			return
		if type.startswith('Radio') or type.startswith('Exclusive'):
			self.updateRadioGroup()
		# if par.eval():
		# 	self.RunOnCommand()
		# else:
		# 	self.RunOffCommand()

	def updateRadioGroup(self):
		"""Turn off other radio buttons

		:return: False if change reverted because trying to turn off Radio,
			otherwise True
		"""
		#debug('radiogroup')
		type = self.buttonTypePar.eval()
		groupButtons = self.GroupButtons
		if type.startswith('Radio') and parent.Widget.par.Value0 == 0 and \
				not any([button.par.Value0 for button in groupButtons]):
			self.SetValue(1)
			return False
		if parent.Widget.par.Value0:
			for button in groupButtons:
				if button != parent.Widget:
					button.par.Value0 = False
		return True

	def onParSettingsChange(self, par, prev):
		if par.name == self.buttonTypePar.name:
			if par.eval().startswith('Momentary'):
				self.onPanelEvent('onToOff')
			elif par.eval().startswith('Radio') \
									or par.eval().startswith('Exclusive'):
				self.updateRadioGroup()
			parent.Widget.par.Buttongroupdat.enable = \
										par.eval().startswith('Radio') \
										or par.eval().startswith('Exclusive')
		else:
			SubWidgetExt.onParSettingsChange(self, par, prev)

*�����������������SubWidgetExt = mod.SubWidgetExt.SubWidgetExt
TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

class ButtonExt(SubWidgetExt):
	"""
	ButtonExt provides all functionality for basic widget button
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.parPrefix = 'Button'
		SubWidgetExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		self.valueIndex = self.ownerComp.digits

		self.types = [
				('Momentary', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.SetValue(1),
							   'onToOff': lambda p: self.SetValue(0)}),
				('Momentary Up', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.SetValue(0),
								  'onToOff': lambda p: self.SetValue(1)}),
				('Toggle Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Toggle()}),
				('Toggle Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Toggle(checkInside=True)}),
				('Toggle Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Toggle()}),
				('Radio Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Radio Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Radio(checkInside=True)}),
				('Radio Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Exclusive Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Exclusive Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Radio(checkInside=True)}),
				('Exclusive Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Commands Only', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.RunOnCommand(),
							   	   'onToOff': lambda p: self.RunOffCommand()}),
				('Commands Only Ignore Value', {
								   'offToOn': lambda p: self.RunOnCommand(),
							   	   'onToOff': lambda p: self.RunOffCommand()})
		]
		self.buttonTypePar = parent.Widget.par.Buttontype
		self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuLabels = [type[0] for type in self.types]
		self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuNames = self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuLabels

	def SetValue(self, value):
		"""
		Sets the value of the widget
		"""
		setattr(self.ownerComp.parent.Widget.par,
										'Value'+str(self.valueIndex), value)

	def GetValue(self):
		"""
		Sets the value of the widget
		"""
		#debug('value change')
		return getattr(self.ownerComp.parent.Widget.par,
										'Value'+str(self.valueIndex))

	def Radio(self, checkInside=False):
		if self.Toggle(checkInside):
			self.updateRadioGroup()

	def Toggle(self, checkInside=False):
		"""
		Sets the value of the button to on when off, and off when on

		:param checkInside: if True, only toggle if the mouse is currently
			inside the ownerComp panel.
		:return: False if skipped from not inside, otherwise True
		"""
		if checkInside and not self.ownerComp.panel.inside:
			return False
		self.SetValue(1-self.GetValue())
		return True

	def RunOnCommand(self):
		"""
		Runs the python command for when the button turns on
		"""
		#debug('command on')
		command = parent.Widget.par.Offtoonscript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
		command = parent.Widget.par.Onvaluechangescript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)

	def RunOffCommand(self):
		"""
		Runs the python command for when the button turns off
		"""
		#debug('command off')
		command = parent.Widget.par.Ontooffscript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
		command = parent.Widget.par.Onvaluechangescript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
	@property
	def GroupButtons(self):
		table = parent.Widget.par.Buttongroupdat.eval()
		if not isinstance(table, DAT):
			return []
		tableParent = table.parent()
		return [tableParent.op(c.val)
							for c in table.col(0) if tableParent.op(c.val)]

	def onPanelEvent(self, event, panelValue=None):
		"""
		Selects correct funtion based on button widget type
		"""
		#debug('panelEvent', event)
		typeIndex = self.buttonTypePar.menuIndex
		btype = self.types[typeIndex][1].get(event)
		#debug(self.types[typeIndex][0])
		if btype:
			#debug('onPanel', event)
			btype(panelValue)

	def onParValueChange(self, par, prev):
		"""
		The Button's value has changed.
		Only runs when necessary (see parexecValue 'Active' parameter).
		"""
		type = self.buttonTypePar.eval()
		if type == 'Commands_Only_Ignore_Value':
			return
		if type.startswith('Radio') or type.startswith('Exclusive'):
			self.updateRadioGroup()
		# if par.eval():
		# 	self.RunOnCommand()
		# else:
		# 	self.RunOffCommand()

	def updateRadioGroup(self):
		"""Turn off other radio buttons

		:return: False if change reverted because trying to turn off Radio,
			otherwise True
		"""
		#debug('radiogroup')
		type = self.buttonTypePar.eval()
		groupButtons = self.GroupButtons
		if type.startswith('Radio') and parent.Widget.par.Value0 == 0 and \
				not any([button.par.Value0 for button in groupButtons]):
			self.SetValue(1)
			return False
		if parent.Widget.par.Value0:
			for button in groupButtons:
				if button != parent.Widget:
					button.par.Value0 = False
		return True

	def onParSettingsChange(self, par, prev):
		if par.name == self.buttonTypePar.name:
			if par.eval().startswith('Momentary'):
				self.onPanelEvent('onToOff')
			elif par.eval().startswith('Radio') \
									or par.eval().startswith('Exclusive'):
				self.updateRadioGroup()
			parent.Widget.par.Buttongroupdat.enable = \
										par.eval().startswith('Radio') \
										or par.eval().startswith('Exclusive')
		else:
			SubWidgetExt.onParSettingsChange(self, par, prev)

*�����������������SubWidgetExt = mod.SubWidgetExt.SubWidgetExt
TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

class ButtonExt(SubWidgetExt):
	"""
	ButtonExt provides all functionality for basic widget button
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.parPrefix = 'Button'
		SubWidgetExt.__init__(self, ownerComp)
		self.valueIndex = self.ownerComp.digits

		self.types = [
				('Momentary', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.SetValue(1),
							   'onToOff': lambda p: self.SetValue(0)}),
				('Momentary Up', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.SetValue(0),
								  'onToOff': lambda p: self.SetValue(1)}),
				('Toggle Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Toggle()}),
				('Toggle Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Toggle(checkInside=True)}),
				('Toggle Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Toggle()}),
				('Radio Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Radio Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Radio(checkInside=True)}),
				('Radio Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Exclusive Down', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Exclusive Up',
				 		{'onToOff': lambda p: self.Radio(checkInside=True)}),
				('Exclusive Up Anywhere', {'onToOff':lambda p: self.Radio()}),
				('Commands Only', {'offToOn': lambda p: self.RunOnCommand(),
							   	   'onToOff': lambda p: self.RunOffCommand()}),
				('Commands Only Ignore Value', {
								   'offToOn': lambda p: self.RunOnCommand(),
							   	   'onToOff': lambda p: self.RunOffCommand()})
		]
		self.buttonTypePar = parent.Widget.par.Buttontype
		self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuLabels = [type[0] for type in self.types]
		self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuNames = self.ownerComp.par.Buttontype.menuLabels

	def SetValue(self, value):
		"""
		Sets the value of the widget
		"""
		setattr(self.ownerComp.parent.Widget.par,
										'Value'+str(self.valueIndex), value)

	def GetValue(self):
		"""
		Sets the value of the widget
		"""
		#debug('value change')
		return getattr(self.ownerComp.parent.Widget.par,
										'Value'+str(self.valueIndex))

	def Radio(self, checkInside=False):
		if self.Toggle(checkInside):
			self.updateRadioGroup()

	def Toggle(self, checkInside=False):
		"""
		Sets the value of the button to on when off, and off when on

		:param checkInside: if True, only toggle if the mouse is currently
			inside the ownerComp panel.
		:return: False if skipped from not inside, otherwise True
		"""
		if checkInside and not self.ownerComp.panel.inside:
			return False
		self.SetValue(1-self.GetValue())
		return True

	def RunOnCommand(self):
		"""
		Runs the python command for when the button turns on
		"""
		#debug('command on')
		command = parent.Widget.par.Offtoonscript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
		command = parent.Widget.par.Onvaluechangescript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)

	def RunOffCommand(self):
		"""
		Runs the python command for when the button turns off
		"""
		#debug('command off')
		command = parent.Widget.par.Ontooffscript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
		command = parent.Widget.par.Onvaluechangescript0.eval()
		if command:
			run(command, fromOP=parent.Widget)
	@property
	def GroupButtons(self):
		table = parent.Widget.par.Buttongroupdat.eval()
		if not isinstance(table, DAT):
			return []
		tableParent = table.parent()
		return [tableParent.op(c.val)
							for c in table.col(0) if tableParent.op(c.val)]

	def onPanelEvent(self, event, panelValue=None):
		"""
		Selects correct funtion based on button widget type
		"""
		#debug('panelEvent', event)
		typeIndex = self.buttonTypePar.menuIndex
		btype = self.types[typeIndex][1].get(event)
		#debug(self.types[typeIndex][0])
		if btype:
			#debug('onPanel', event)
			btype(panelValue)

	def onParValueChange(self, par, prev):
		"""
		The Button's value has changed.
		Only runs when necessary (see parexecValue 'Active' parameter).
		"""
		type = self.buttonTypePar.eval()
		if type == 'Commands_Only_Ignore_Value':
			return
		if type.startswith('Radio') or type.startswith('Exclusive'):
			self.updateRadioGroup()
		# if par.eval():
		# 	self.RunOnCommand()
		# else:
		# 	self.RunOffCommand()

	def updateRadioGroup(self):
		"""Turn off other radio buttons

		:return: False if change reverted because trying to turn off Radio,
			otherwise True
		"""
		#debug('radiogroup')
		type = self.buttonTypePar.eval()
		groupButtons = self.GroupButtons
		if type.startswith('Radio') and parent.Widget.par.Value0 == 0 and \
				not any([button.par.Value0 for button in groupButtons]):
			self.SetValue(1)
			return False
		if parent.Widget.par.Value0:
			for button in groupButtons:
				if button != parent.Widget:
					button.par.Value0 = False
		return True

	def onParSettingsChange(self, par, prev):
		if par.name == self.buttonTypePar.name:
			if par.eval().startswith('Momentary'):
				self.onPanelEvent('onToOff')
			elif par.eval().startswith('Radio') \
									or par.eval().startswith('Exclusive'):
				self.updateRadioGroup()
			parent.Widget.par.Buttongroupdat.enable = \
										par.eval().startswith('Radio') \
										or par.eval().startswith('Exclusive')
		else:
			SubWidgetExt.onParSettingsChange(self, par, prev)

*�����������������"""
Extension classes enhance TouchDesigner components with python. An
extension is accessed via ext.ExtensionClassName from any operator
within the extended component. If the extension is promoted via its
Promote Extension parameter, all its attributes with capitalized names
can be accessed externally, e.g. op('yourComp').PromotedFunction().

Help: search "Extensions" in wiki
"""

from TDStoreTools import StorageManager
TDF = op.TDModules.mod.TDFunctions

class SynchroCacheExt:
	"""
	SynchroCacheExt description
	"""
	def __init__(self, ownerComp):
		# The component to which this extension is attached
		self.ownerComp = ownerComp
		self.syncChop = self.ownerComp.op('syncin1')
		self.fifoDat = self.ownerComp.op('fifo1')
		self.fifoPlayedDat = self.ownerComp.op('fifo_playedIndex')
		self.statsDat = self.ownerComp.op('stats')
		# properties

		#The index into the frame cache that matches to the sync frame
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'FrameCacheIndex', value=None, dependable=True,
							readOnly=0)

		#The index of the last synced frame
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'LastSyncFrame', value=0, dependable=True,
							readOnly=False)

		#The index of the last synced frame that was found in the cache
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'LastValidSyncFrame', value=0, dependable=True,
							readOnly=False)

		#A sync frame was received but a matching frame in the cache was not found
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'MissedSyncFrames', value=0, dependable=True,
							readOnly=False)

		#The index of the last cached frame
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'LastCachedFrame', value=0, dependable=True,
							readOnly=False)

		#The number of times the same frame index was passed into the receiver
		#This would mean a dropped frame between server and receiver CHOP
		TDF.createProperty(self, 'FrameRepeats', value=0, dependable=True,
							readOnly=False)

		self.cacheSize = self.ownerComp.par.Cachesize.eval()

	def frameCache(self, frame):
		"""
		Records a new incoming frame index from the incoming video stream to the fifo DAT and updates cache stats
		"""
		self.ownerComp.op('fifo1').appendRow([frame])
		self.LastCachedFrame = frame		

	def frameRead(self, syncFrame):
		"""
		Handler for a new sync frame. This function searches for requested frame in the frame cache and will select the frame 
		for display through the cacheSelect via FrameCacheIndex property
		"""
		#')
		frame = int(syncFrame)
		#debug('sf', syncFrame, self.getCacheIndex(frame))
		cacheIndex = self.getCacheIndex(frame)
		if cacheIndex:
			cacheLookup = -(self.cacheSize - cacheIndex)
			self.FrameCacheIndex = cacheLookup
			self.LastSyncFrame = frame
			self.statsDat[1, 'LastPlayedFrame'] = syncFrame 
		else:
			self.statsDat[1, 'MissedFrames'] = self.MissedSyncFrames

	def getCacheIndex(self, frame):
		"""
		Returns the index for the frame in the cache
		"""
		if frame is not None:
			cacheIndexCells = self.fifoDat.findCells(str(frame))
			#debug(cacheIndexCell, frame)
			if cacheIndexCells:
				if len(cacheIndexCells) > 1:
					self.FrameRepeats = self.FrameRepeats + 1
				index = cacheIndexCells[0].row
				#debug('row', index, self.fifoDat[index,0].val)
				#self.statsDat[1, 'LastPlayedFrame'] = self.MissedSyncFrames
				return cacheIndexCells[0].row
			else:
				self.MissedSyncFrames = self.MissedSyncFrames + 1
				self.statsDat[1, 'MissedFrames'] = self.MissedSyncFrames
				return self.fifoDat.findCell(str(self.LastValidSyncFrame))

	def viewStats(self):
		"""
		Opens the viewer of the main info out chop
		"""
		self.ownerComp.op('out2').openViewer()

	def resetStats(self):
		"""
		Opens the viewer of the main info out chop
		"""
		self.MissedSyncFrames = 0
		self.LastCachedFrame = 0
		self.FrameCacheIndex = 0
		self.LastSyncFrame = 0
		self.SyncFrameLatency = 0

	def frames2Timecode(self, frame):
		"""
		Converts frame to timecode
		"""
		fps = me.time.rate
		h = int(frame / (me.time.rate*60*60))
		m = int(frame / (me.time.rate*60)) % 60
		s = int((frame % (me.time.rate*60))/me.time.rate)
		f = int(frame % (me.time.rate*60) % me.time.rate)
		tc = ( "%02d:%02d:%02d:%02d" % ( h, m, s, f))
		return tc
